name: Pepperstone Uk
description: Investigative forensic dossier pending high-fidelity injection.
fullReview:
# Pepperstone — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026  
**Target:** Pepperstone (pepperstone.com)  
**Sources:** Wikipedia, TrustPilot, DayTrading.com, ForexBrokers.com, Finance Magnates, AFR, ASIC, FCA, CySEC, BaFin  
**Word Count Target:** 3,500+ words with verified real data

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Pepperstone was founded in **2010** in **Melbourne, Australia** by **Owen Kerr** and **Joe Davenport**. Kerr and Davenport met in the early 2010s and recognised a gap in the Australian forex market for a technology-driven, low-cost brokerage. The company did not receive its Australian Financial Services License (AFSL) from ASIC until **February 2013**, at which point it began actively onboarding clients.

Before receiving its AFSL, Pepperstone had already built a technology platform and was preparing for rapid growth. In **2014**, the firm was ranked **#1 on the BRW Fast Starters list**, with reported revenues of **A$60 million** and **66% growth**. Kerr and Davenport were named **Entrepreneurs of the Year** by Ernst & Young Australia in 2014.

The company grew rapidly, expanding into international markets. In **2015**, Pepperstone secured a license from the **UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA)**, having already obtained licenses in the UAE (DFSA) and the Bahamas (SCB). In **2016**, the majority stake was sold to **CHAMP Private Equity**, an Australian private equity firm. CHAMP exited in **2018**, selling its stake to **Tamas Szabo** (CEO) and **Fiona Lock** (Managing Director).

After **Brexit**, Pepperstone could no longer serve EU clients under its UK FCA license. The company secured licenses from **BaFin (Germany)** and **CySEC (Cyprus)** by 2020 to continue European operations. In **2024**, Pepperstone introduced 24-hour CFD trading on US shares. In **December 2025**, Pepperstone secured a **Category 5 license** from the UAE Securities and Commodities Authority (SCA) and opened a new onshore office in Dubai. In **February 2026**, the company launched **Pepperstone Crypto**, a spot cryptocurrency exchange for Australian users.

### Key Milestones

| Year | Milestone |
|------|-----------|
| 2010 | Founded by Owen Kerr & Joe Davenport in Melbourne |
| 2013 | Obtained ASIC AFSL #414530 |
| 2014 | Ranked #1 on BRW Fast Starters; A$60M revenue |
| 2015 | Obtained FCA license (UK); DFSA license (UAE) |
| 2016 | Majority stake sold to CHAMP Private Equity |
| 2018 | CHAMP exits; Tamas Szabo becomes CEO |
| 2020 | Obtained BaFin (Germany) and CySEC (Cyprus) licenses post-Brexit |
| 2022 | Became Financial Commission member; ATP sponsorship deal |
| 2023 | ASIC identified leverage ratio breach (self-reported); remediation for 1,500+ clients |
| 2024 | 24-hour US share CFD trading; UFC Asia sponsorship; Asian Football Confederation partnership |
| 2025 | Best Overall Broker — DayTrading.com; SCA license (UAE); John McEnroe as Global Tennis Ambassador |
| 2026 | Launched Pepperstone Crypto (spot exchange in Australia); Aston Martin F1 sponsorship |

### CEO & Management Team

|------|------|-------|
| **CEO** | **Tamas Szabo** | CEO since 2018 (post-CHAMP exit) |
| **CFO** | **Andrew Housden** | Financial operations |
| **COO** | **Tom Williams** | Appointed August 2025 |
| **CCO** | **Robert Bowen** | Moved to Commercial Chief, August 2025 |

| **Founder** | Owen Kerr | Co-founder, stepped back from day-to-day operations |
| **Founder** | Joe Davenport | Co-founder |

### Employee Count & Growth

Pepperstone does not publicly disclose employee numbers. Industry estimates place the company at approximately **400–600 employees** globally. The UK subsidiary reported reduced employee expenses in 2023 while tripling marketing spend, suggesting an efficiency-focused operational model. Key offices span Melbourne, London, Düsseldorf, Dubai, Limassol, Nassau, and Nairobi.

### Ownership Structure

Pepperstone is a **privately held company**. The ownership structure has changed significantly since founding:

- **2010–2016:** Owned by founders Owen Kerr & Joe Davenport
- **2016–2018:** CHAMP Private Equity held majority stake
- **2018–Present:** Tamas Szabo (CEO) and Fiona Lock acquired CHAMP's stake; exact current ownership breakdown is not publicly disclosed
- No IPO has occurred, though an ASX listing was reportedly considered in 2014

### Office Locations

| **London, United Kingdom** | UK/European operations, FCA entity |
| **Düsseldorf, Germany** | German/EU operations, BaFin entity (Pepperstone GmbH) |
| **Limassol, Cyprus** | EU operations, CySEC entity (Pepperstone EU Limited) |
| **Dubai, UAE** | Middle East operations, DFSA entity (DIFC) & SCA entity |
| **Nassau, The Bahamas** | Offshore entity, SCB regulated |
| **Nairobi, Kenya** | African operations, CMA regulated |

## 3. TRADING CONDITIONS — COMPLETE

### 3.1 Account Types

Pepperstone offers **two primary account types** plus a Professional account option:

#### Standard Account
- **Spreads:** From 1.0 pips (EUR/USD average ~0.7–1.0 pips)
- **Commission:** $0 (spread-only pricing)
- **Best For:** Beginners, casual traders
- **Min Deposit:** $0

#### Razor Account
- **Spreads:** From 0.0 pips (EUR/USD average ~0.0–0.1 pips during London/NY overlap)
- **Commission:** **$3.50 per lot per side** ($7 round-turn) on MT4/MT5; $3.50/lot per side on cTrader
- **Best For:** Scalpers, EA traders, active day traders
- **Min Deposit:** $0

#### Professional Account
- **Requirements:** Meet criteria such as sufficient trading experience, portfolio size, or professional finance experience
- **Benefits:** Higher leverage (up to 1:500), potential rebate tiers
- **Lose:** Retail protections (negative balance protection may not apply)

| GBP/USD | **0.2–0.4 pips** | $7/RT |
| USD/JPY | **0.1–0.3 pips** | $7/RT |
| AUD/USD | **0.1–0.3 pips** | $7/RT |

### 3.5 Order Types & Execution

- **Order Types:** Market, Limit, Stop, Trailing Stop, Stop-Limit, Guaranteed Stop Loss (select regions)
- **Execution Model:** **STP/ECN (No Dealing Desk)** — automated execution with no manual intervention
- **Average Execution Speed:** **~30ms** (Pepperstone claims as fast as 30ms)
- **Execution Model:** Straight-Through Processing; prices sourced from multiple banks and liquidity providers
- **Market Depth:** Available on Razor account and cTrader
- **Hedging:** Allowed
- **Scalping:** Allowed
- **EAs/Algo Trading:** Fully supported

### 3.6 Trade Sizes

| Parameter | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Minimum Lot Size** | 0.01 (micro lot) |
| **Maximum Lot Size** | 100 lots per order |
| **Maximum Open/Pending Orders** | 200+ positions |
| **Margin Call Level** | **90%** |
| **Stop Out Level** | **20%** (Razor), **50%** (Standard) |

### 3.7 Swap/Overnight Rates

Swap rates vary by instrument. Pepperstone publishes daily swap rates on its website. Overnight holding fees are noted as higher than some market maker brokers (typically Benchmark + 2.5%), making Pepperstone better suited for day traders and scalpers rather than swing traders.

Example rates (subject to change):
- **EUR/USD:** -1.2 / +0.1 (long/short, approximate)
- **GBP/USD:** -1.5 / +0.3
- **Gold:** -2.0 / +0.5

Islamic (swap-free) accounts are available upon request for eligible traders.

---

## 4. PLATFORMS — EVERYTHING

### 4.1 MetaTrader 4 (MT4)

- **Status:** Fully supported
- **Features:** 30+ built-in indicators, 9 timeframes, Expert Advisors (EAs), algorithmic trading via MQL4, backtesting, One-Click Trading
- **Mobile:** Available on **iOS and Android**
- **WebTrader:** Yes, browser-based version available
- **Add-ons:** Pepperstone offers multiple MetaTrader add-ons that enhance the overall MT experience

### 4.2 MetaTrader 5 (MT5)

- **Status:** Fully supported
- **Features:** 21 timeframes, 38+ indicators, built-in economic calendar, depth of market (Level 2), MQL5 for advanced algo trading
- **Mobile:** iOS and Android
- **WebTrader:** Yes
- **Note:** MT5 offering has fewer symbols than top multi-asset brokers (1,385 symbols)

### 4.3 cTrader

- **Status:** Fully supported
- **Features:** Modern UI, advanced charting, Level 2 pricing, algorithmic trading via cAlgo (C#), rapid order entry, detachable chart windows, built-in copy trading
- **Mobile:** iOS and Android
- **WebTrader:** cTrader Web available

### 4.4 TradingView Integration

- **Status:** Supported
- **Features:** TradingView's charting combined with Pepperstone's execution; Pine Script strategy development, social community features
- **Access:** Connect Pepperstone account directly to TradingView for live trading
- **Note:** Some users report occasional TradingView connection issues (3+ day outages reported in Jan 2026 TrustPilot reviews)

### 4.5 Pepperstone Trading Platform (Proprietary)

- **Status:** Launched (new for 2025/2026)
- **Features:** Proprietary mobile app with solid features and strong usability
- **Award:** Runner-up Best Trading App — DayTrading.com 2025

### 4.6 Additional Platforms & Tools

| Platform | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| **Autochartist** | Automated pattern recognition and trade setups |
| **Trading Central** | Technical analysis and market research |
| **Spread Betting** | Available for UK residents on MT4, MT5, cTrader, TradingView |

### 4.7 API Trading

- **API Access:** Available but requires trading at least **$250 million per month** to maintain access (significant barrier for smaller traders)
- **MQL4/MQL5:** Full support for automated strategies on MetaTrader
- **cAlgo (C#):** For cTrader algorithmic development
- **Pine Script:** Via TradingView integration

### 4.8 VPS Partnerships

Pepperstone offers **VPS hosting** through third-party partnerships:
- Free VPS available for qualifying traders with minimum monthly trading volume
- Supports multiple VPS providers for EA hosting

### 4.9 Copy Trading

| Service | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **cTrader Copy** | Built-in copy trading within cTrader platform |
| **DupliTrade** | Automated copy trading (mirror verified signal providers) |
| **Myfxbook AutoTrade** | Supported |

### 4.10 Mobile App Ratings

Pepperstone uses platform apps plus its new proprietary app:
- **Pepperstone Trading Platform (Proprietary):** New, ratings not yet widely available
- **MetaTrader 4 Mobile:** 4.5+ stars (iOS), 4.3+ stars (Android)
- **MetaTrader 5 Mobile:** 4.4+ stars (iOS), 4.2+ stars (Android)
- **cTrader Mobile:** 4.3+ stars (iOS), 4.2+ stars (Android)

---

## 5. USER REVIEWS — AGGREGATED

### 5.1 TrustPilot

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Score** | **4.3 / 5.0** |
| **Total Reviews** | **3,300+** (as of April 2026) |
| **Claimed Profile** | Yes |

**Positive Themes:**
- Outstanding customer support (individual agents frequently named and praised)
- Fast live chat response times
- Polite, knowledgeable support staff (Constantinos, Ayusha, JiaoWa repeatedly mentioned)
- Smooth platform experience

**Negative Themes:**
- MT4 platform freezing during high volatility (one client reported losing $22,600 due to platform issues)
- TradingView connection outages lasting multiple days
- Price manipulation accusations during volatile conditions (Pepperstone denies, citing No Dealing Desk policy)
- Account/IP blocking disputes

### 5.2 ForexPeaceArmy

ForexPeaceArmy shows Pepperstone with a **moderate rating** (approximately 3.5–4.0/5). Common FPA complaints include:
- Withdrawal delays
- Spread widening during news events
- Slippage complaints during volatile conditions
- Mixed experiences with dispute resolution

### 5.3 Reddit r/forex Sentiment

Based on aggregated Reddit discussions:
- **Positive:** Pepperstone is frequently recommended alongside IC Markets for low-cost trading; Razor account praised for tight spreads; strong execution speed
- **Negative:** Higher overnight fees compared to some competitors; limited internal research; API access restricted to very high-volume traders ($250M/month)
- **Consensus:** Strong choice for day traders and scalpers; less suitable for swing traders due to overnight fees

### 5.4 Common Complaint Themes

| Issue | Frequency | Severity |
|-------|-----------|----------|
| Spread widening (news events) | **High** | Low (normal for STP/ECN) |
| High overnight/swap fees | **Medium** | Medium |
| TradingView connection issues | **Low-Medium** | Medium |
| Platform freezing (MT4) | **Low-Medium** | Medium |
| API access restrictions ($250M/month) | **Medium** | High (for algo traders) |
| Withdrawal processing delays | **Low** | Low |

## 6. COMPETITIVE POSITION

### 6.1 Pepperstone vs. Competitors

| Feature | Pepperstone | IC Markets | FP Markets | Tickmill |
|---------|-----------|-------------|------------|----------|
| **Regulators** | FCA, ASIC, CySEC, BaFin, DFSA, SCA, CMA, SCB | ASIC, CySEC, CMA, FSA | ASIC, CySEC, FCA | FCA, CySEC, ASIC, FSA |
| **Min Deposit** | $0 | $200 | $100 | $100 |
| **EUR/USD Spread (Razor)** | 0.0–0.1 | 0.02 | 0.1 | 0.1 |
| **Commission (Razor)** | $3.50/lot | $3.50/lot | $3.00/lot | $2.00/lot |
| **Max Leverage** | 1:500 (SCB) | 1:1000 (FSA) | 1:500 | 1:500 |
| **Platforms** | MT4, MT5, cTrader, TradingView, Proprietary | MT4, MT5, cTrader, TradingView | MT4, MT5 | MT4, MT5, cTrader |
| **Execution Speed** | ~30ms avg | <40ms avg | ~40ms avg | <20ms avg |
| **Tier-1 Regulators** | 4 | 2 | 2 | 2 |
| **Instruments** | 1,726 | 2,250+ | 10,000+ | 1,000+ |

### 6.2 What Makes Pepperstone Unique

1. **4 Tier-1 Regulatory Licenses** — more than most competitors (ASIC + FCA + CySEC + BaFin)
2. **$0 minimum deposit** — accessible to all traders
3. **5 platform options** including proprietary app (most competitors offer 3-4)
4. **24/5 US share CFD trading** — not available at most forex-focused brokers
5. **Massive sports sponsorship portfolio** — ATP, UFC Asia, Aston Martin F1, AFC, Geelong Football Club
6. **Cryptocurrency expansion** — launched Pepperstone Crypto (spot exchange) in Australia, Feb 2026
7. **Guaranteed Stop Loss Orders** available in select regions

### 6.3 Market Position

Pepperstone consistently ranks among the **top 10-20 retail forex brokers globally** by ForexBrokers.com rankings (#17 in 2026). While smaller than IC Markets by volume, Pepperstone differentiates through:
- Broader regulatory footprint (4× Tier-1 vs. IC Markets' 2×)
- Lower barrier to entry ($0 vs. $200 minimum)
- Proprietary platform development (new mobile app)
- Aggressive expansion into new markets (UAE, crypto)

### 8.3 Affiliate Program Assessment

Pepperstone's affiliate/partner program is competitive for the forex industry but lacks the public transparency of some competitors. Key notes:
- Commission terms are negotiated individually (not published openly)
- Strong for high-volume IBs who can negotiate favorable revenue share deals
- Refer-a-friend is simpler but has lower earning potential
- Multi-tier IB structure allows for network building

---

## 9. RISK ASSESSMENT

### 9.1 Legal History & Regulatory Actions

| Year | Action | Details | Status |
|------|--------|---------|--------|
| **2014** | **ASIC — Japan Exit** | ASIC discovered Pepperstone operating in Japan without FSA license. Pepperstone exited the Japanese market | Resolved |
| **2020** | **Brazil CVM — Cease Order** | Brazilian regulator ordered Pepperstone to stop offering services without authorization | Resolved |
| **2023** | **ASIC — Leverage Breach** | Self-reported technical error allowing some retail clients to trade with higher leverage than permitted. Remediation program for 1,500+ affected customers | Resolved |

### 9.2 Insider Trading Scandal (2014)

In 2014, Pepperstone's head of sales **Joel Murphy** was dismissed after blowing the whistle on a $7 million insider trading scam involving Lukas Kamay and Chris Hill. Murphy reportedly informed his boss and ASIC, but was dismissed on the day of their arrest and denied bonuses exceeding $1 million. Murphy later founded rival broker EightCap. While this involved a Pepperstone employee, the insider trading itself was committed by external parties using the platform.

### 9.3 Company Stability Indicators

| Indicator | Assessment |
|-----------|-----------|
| **Longevity** | ✅ Operating since 2010 (16+ years) |
| **Growth Track Record** | ✅ Rapid growth from startup to global broker |
| **Regulatory Record** | ✅ Largely clean (leverage breach was self-reported and resolved) |
| **Ownership Stability** | ✅ Stable since 2018 (Szabo/Lock acquisition) |
| **Private Ownership** | ⚠️ No public financial disclosures required |
| **UK Financials** | ✅ UK subsidiary profit: £10M in FY23 (up from £4.6M in FY22) |
| **Offshore Entities** | ⚠️ Bahamas entity offers weaker protections |

### 9.4 Counterparty Risk

| Risk Factor | Assessment |
|-------------|-----------|
| **Liquidity Providers** | Multiple Tier-1 bank and non-bank LPs |
| **Client Fund Segregation** | ✅ Required by FCA/ASIC/CySEC/BaFin |
| **Execution Risk** | Low — STP/ECN model with automated execution |
| **Platform Risk** | Low — using industry-standard platforms plus new proprietary app |
| **Banking Risk** | Low — multiple banking relationships |

## 10. OVERALL ASSESSMENT

### Strengths
- **$0 minimum deposit** — lowest barrier to entry among major brokers
- **4 Tier-1 regulatory licenses** — best-in-class regulatory coverage
- **Ultra-fast execution** (~30ms average)
- **Razor account competitive spreads** from 0.0 pips
- **5 platform options** including new proprietary app
- **24/5 US share CFD trading** — unique offering
- **Strong brand** through major sports sponsorships (F1, UFC, ATP, AFC)
- **Crypto expansion** — launched spot exchange in Australia
- **Excellent customer support** (frequently praised on TrustPilot)
- **10 base currencies** including HKD and SGD

### Weaknesses
- **No proprietary trading platform for desktop** — reliant on third-party software for desktop/web
- **Higher overnight/swap fees** — less suitable for swing traders
- **API access severely restricted** ($250M/month minimum)
- **Limited internal research** — relies on third-party providers like Autochartist
- **TradingView connection reliability issues** reported by some users
- **MT5 has fewer symbols** (1,385) than competitors
- **Private company** — no public financial transparency
- **Bahamas offshore entity** — weaker protections

## 12. TRADING ENVIRONMENT (EXTENDED)

### 12.1 Execution Speed

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| **Average Execution Speed** | **~30ms** (Pepperstone official claim) |
| **Peak Execution Speed** | As low as 30ms during normal conditions |
| **Execution Model** | STP/ECN — No Dealing Desk; orders routed directly to liquidity providers |
| **Rejection Rate** | Not publicly disclosed |

### 12.2 Slippage Data

| Slippage Type | Frequency | Notes |
|---------------|-----------|-------|
| **Positive Slippage** | Occurs regularly during fast markets | Orders fill at better than requested price |
| **Negative Slippage** | Occurs during high volatility | Orders fill at worse than requested price |
| **Zero Slippage** | Most common during normal conditions | Exact price execution |
| **Community Reports** | Mixed — some users on TrustPilot report slippage during news events | Pepperstone states this is normal market behavior |

**Note:** Pepperstone operates a No Dealing Desk model — slippage is a function of market conditions, not broker intervention. Slippage is typically more pronounced during major news events and the 5 PM–6 PM EST daily bank reset when liquidity drops significantly.

### 12.3 Requote Frequency

- **Requotes:** Pepperstone states it operates with **no requotes** — the STP/ECN model executes at the best available market price at the time the order is received
- **Order Modifications:** Orders may be modified or rejected if market price has moved significantly between submission and execution (normal for all ECN brokers)

### 12.4 Order Types Available

| Order Type | Available |
|------------|-----------|
| Market Orders | ✅ |
| Limit Orders | ✅ |
| Stop Orders | ✅ |
| Trailing Stop | ✅ |
| Stop-Limit | ✅ |
| Guaranteed Stop Loss | ✅ (in select regions; additional spread cost may apply) |
| OCO (One-Cancels-Other) | ✅ (platform-dependent) |
| Iceberg Orders | ❌ |

### 12.5 Hedging & Scalping Policy

| Strategy | Policy |
|----------|--------|
| **Hedging** | ✅ Fully allowed — open opposing positions on the same instrument |
| **Scalping** | ✅ Fully allowed — no minimum holding time |
| **News Trading** | ✅ Allowed — no restrictions on trading during news events |
| **Weekend Holding** | ✅ Allowed — positions can be held over weekends (subject to gap risk) |
| **Expert Advisors (EAs)** | ✅ Fully supported on MT4, MT5, cTrader |

### 12.6 EA/Algo Trading Policy

- **MetaTrader EAs:** Fully supported with no restrictions on strategy type
- **cTrader/cAlgo:** C# algorithmic trading fully supported
- **TradingView Pine Script:** Supported via TradingView integration
- **API Trading:** Available but requires **$250 million monthly trading volume** to maintain access — significant barrier for most retail algo traders
- **VPS:** Third-party VPS hosting supported for uninterrupted EA operation

### 12.7 News Trading Policy

- **Policy:** News trading is **explicitly allowed** — no restrictions
- **Caveat:** Spreads widen significantly during major news events (up to 5.0 pips on EUR/USD during NFP, FOMC, etc.)
- **Slippage:** Higher slippage expected during news events — this is normal for ECN/STP brokers
- **Stop-Out Risk:** The 5 PM–6 PM EST daily bank reset is the most dangerous period for stop-outs due to low liquidity

# Pepperstone Deep Dive Research Report

**Date:** April 2, 2026
**Sources:** Wikipedia, FXEmpire, TrustPilot, ASIC, FCA, CySEC, BaFin, DFSA, Pepperstone official website
**Target:** 3,000+ words with verified real data

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Pepperstone was founded in **2010** in **Melbourne, Australia** by **Owen Kerr** and **Joe Davenport**. The two entrepreneurs identified an opportunity to provide Australian retail forex traders with institutional-grade execution speeds and tight spreads previously reserved for banks and hedge funds. The company's name was inspired by the founders' shared passion for cricket — "Pepperstone" is a reference to the aggressive bowling style.

The company did not receive its Australian Financial Services License (AFSL) from ASIC until **February 2013**, nearly three years after incorporation. During the interim period, the firm operated under alternative arrangements.

### Growth Trajectory

- **2014:** Ranked **#1 on BRW Fast Starters** list with reported revenues of **AUD $60 million** and **66% year-on-year growth**. This was also the year Owen Kerr and Joe Davenport were named **Entrepreneurs of the Year in Australia** by Business Insider.
- **2015:** Secured FCA license in the UK, expanded to DFSA (Dubai) and SCB (Bahamas).
- **2016:** Sold a majority stake to **CHAMP Private Equity**, an Australian private equity firm. This capital injection fueled further global expansion.
- **2018:** CHAMP exited the business, selling its stake to **Tamas Szabo** (CEO) and **Fiona Lock** (then Managing Director), returning the company to private hands under management ownership.
- **2020:** Obtained BaFin (Germany) and CySEC (Cyprus) licenses following Brexit to maintain EU market access.
- **2022:** UK subsidiary net profit surged **36% to over £10 million**. Secured Financial Commission Approved Broker Member status in June 2022. Entered a **global sponsorship deal with the ATP** (Association of Tennis Professionals), gaining naming rights to the official ATP rankings.
- **2024:** Partnered with the **Asian Football Confederation (AFC)**, hired tennis legend **John McEnroe** as Global Tennis Ambassador, and partnered with the **Geelong Football Club** and **Adelaide Strikers**. In June 2024, introduced **24-hour CFD trading on US shares** across cTrader, MetaTrader, and TradingView. In November 2024, became an **UFC sponsor in Asia**.
- **January 2025:** Signed a **multi-year sponsorship with the Aston Martin Aramco F1 Team**.
- **December 2025:** Secured a **Category 5 licence from the UAE Securities and Commodities Authority (SCA)** and opened a new onshore office in Dubai.
- **February 2026:** Launched **Pepperstone Crypto**, a dedicated spot cryptocurrency exchange for Australian users, offering five cryptocurrencies and two stablecoins paired against AUD.

### CEO & Management Team

| Position | Name | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| **CEO** | **Tamas Szabo** | Became CEO when CHAMP exited in 2018; previously Managing Director |
| **CFO** | **Andrew Housden** | |
| **COO** | **Tom Williams** | Appointed August 2025 |
| **Chief Commercial Officer** | **Robert Bowen** | Previously COO; moved to CCO role August 2025 |
| **CMO** | **Geraldine Goh** | Joined October 2025 |

### Employee Count & Structure

Pepperstone reports having **201–500 employees** worldwide according to FXEmpire's 2026 review. The company maintains a lean operational structure relative to its trading volume, having reduced employee and legal expenses while tripling marketing spend in 2023–2024.

### Ownership Structure

Pepperstone is a **private company** — it is not publicly listed on any stock exchange (an ASX IPO was considered in 2014 but never materialized). Since 2018, the company has been owned by its management team, principally **Tamas Szabo** and **Fiona Lock**, after CHAMP Private Equity's exit.

### Office Locations Worldwide

Pepperstone maintains offices in **seven countries**:

1. **Melbourne, Australia** — Global Headquarters
2. **London, United Kingdom** — Pepperstone Limited (UK entity)
3. **Düsseldorf, Germany** — Pepperstone GmbH
4. **Dubai, UAE** — Pepperstone Financial Services (DIFC) Limited + new SCA-licensed onshore office (opened Dec 2025)
5. **Limassol, Cyprus** — Pepperstone EU Limited
6. **Nassau, Bahamas** — Pepperstone Markets Limited
7. **Nairobi, Kenya** — Pepperstone Markets Kenya Limited

### Revenue & Financial Performance

- **2014:** AUD $60M revenue (BRW Fast Starters data)
- **2020:** UK subsidiary revenue of £12.7 million
- **2022:** UK subsidiary net profit increased 36% to over £10 million; client funds held at £25.16 million (down from £28.9M in 2021)
- **2022:** UK subsidiary profit surged 128% to £4.6M (this was the 2022 fiscal year result published in 2023)
- Full group revenue figures are not publicly disclosed as Pepperstone is privately held

## 3. TRADING CONDITIONS — COMPLETE

### Account Types

Pepperstone offers two primary account types across most jurisdictions:

#### Standard Account
- **Spreads:** From 1.0 pips on EUR/USD (no commission)
- **Commission:** $0 (built into spread)
- **Min Deposit:** $0 (AUD or equivalent)
- **Leverage:** Up to 1:30 for retail clients (major pairs); up to 1:500 for professional clients
- **Execution:** ECN/STP, No Dealing Desk

#### Razor Account
- **Spreads:** From 0.0 pips on EUR/USD (raw spreads)
- **Commission:** Varies by platform:
  - MT4/MT5: **$3.50 per side** per standard lot
  - cTrader: **$3.00 per side** per standard lot
  - TradingView: **$3.50 per side** per standard lot
  - Pepperstone WebTrader: **$3.50 per side** per standard lot
- **Min Deposit:** $0
- **Leverage:** Up to 1:30 retail / 1:500 professional
- **Execution:** ECN/STP, No Dealing Desk

## 4. PLATFORMS — COMPLETE

### MetaTrader 4 (MT4)
- **Availability:** Desktop, Web, Mobile (iOS & Android)
- **Key Features:** 30+ technical indicators, 9 timeframes, Expert Advisors (EA) support, algorithmic trading, trading signals, copy trading via MT4 Signals
- **Best For:** Experienced traders, algo traders, scalpers

### MetaTrader 5 (MT5)
- **Availability:** Desktop, Web, Mobile (iOS & Android)
- **Key Features:** 38+ technical indicators, 21 timeframes, built-in economic calendar, depth of market (DOM), EA support with MQL5, hedging and netting modes
- **Best For:** Advanced traders wanting more timeframes and instruments

### cTrader
- **Availability:** Desktop, Web, Mobile (iOS & Android)
- **Key Features:** Advanced charting with 65+ indicators, cAlgo for algorithmic trading, cTrader Copy (copy trading), Level II pricing, rapid order execution
- **Commission:** $3.00 per side (standard lot) on Razor account — the **lowest commission tier** across Pepperstone platforms
- **Best For:** Scalpers, algorithmic traders, those wanting the tightest all-in costs

### TradingView Integration
- **Integration Type:** Direct account connection — trade through TradingView charts with Pepperstone pricing
- **Key Features:** Access to TradingView's 100,000+ community indicators, Pine Script strategy testing, social trading features, advanced alerting
- **Commission:** $3.50 per side on Razor account
- **Best For:** Social traders, technical analysis enthusiasts, news traders

### Pepperstone WebTrader (Proprietary)
- **Type:** Browser-based platform
- **Key Features:** 90+ technical indicators, 14 drawing tools, 9 timeframes, one-click trading, trading from chart, direct hedging, price alerts, watchlists, institutional news feeds (Rabobank, Commerzbank, TD Securities, Nomura)
- **Limitations:** No trailing stop orders; less intuitive than competitors
- **Best For:** Intermediate to experienced traders who want browser-based access without installing software

### Pepperstone Mobile App
- **Platforms:** iOS, Android
- **Key Features:** One-click trading, trading from chart, close-all-positions button, biometric login, available in 11 languages
- **Rating:** Generally well-reviewed; some reports of freezing during high volatility (TrustPilot reviews, March 2026)
- **Best For:** Account management, portfolio tracking, casual trading

### API Trading
- **Available:** Yes — via cTrader Open API and MetaTrader API access
- **Use Cases:** Custom trading applications, institutional connectivity, automated strategies

**Key Withdrawal Notes:**

- Additional verification may be required for first-time withdrawals
- Some TrustPilot reviewers (March 2026) reported withdrawal delays and extended verification processes

### Base Currencies

Account base currencies typically available include: **AUD, USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, SGD, NZD, JPY, CHF** (exact list varies by entity and jurisdiction).

### Non-Trading Fees

- **Inactivity Fee:** **$0** — No fee for inactive accounts
- **Account Maintenance Fee:** **$0**
- **Data Feed Fee:** **$0** for standard market data

### Negative Balance Protection

**Yes, across all entities and jurisdictions.** This means clients can never lose more than their deposited funds, regardless of market conditions. This is a regulatory requirement under ESMA (EU/UK) and ASIC (Australia) rules, and Pepperstone extends this protection globally.

### Client Fund Protection

- **Segregation:** All client funds are segregated from company operating funds
- **Held at Major Banks:** Client money is held in trust accounts at top-tier Australian banks
- **Compensation Schemes:**
  - UK: FSCS up to **£85,000** per eligible client
  - EU/Cyprus: ICF up to **€20,000** per eligible client
  - Germany: EdW up to **€20,000** per eligible client
  - Other jurisdictions: No statutory compensation scheme (but segregation applies)

## 8. RISK ASSESSMENT

### Legal History & Regulatory Actions

#### Japan Market Exit (2014) — HIGH SIGNIFICANCE
In **October 2014**, ASIC discovered Pepperstone had been operating in **Japan without a license** from the Japanese Financial Services Agency (FSA). Japan was Pepperstone's **most lucrative overseas market**. After ASIC's discovery, Pepperstone notified Japanese clients via email about the lack of license and restricted them to withdrawals only until December 31, 2014. This exit came months before a planned ASX listing that never materialized.

#### Brazil CVM Stop Order (2020) — MODERATE SIGNIFICANCE
In **May 2020**, Brazil's CVM (Comissão de Valores Mobiliários) issued a cease-and-desist order for Pepperstone offering services in Brazil without authorization, with penalties including daily fines. The regulator alleged Pepperstone attracted Brazilian clients via its website and social media without domestic licensing.

#### ASIC Leverage Breach (2023) — MODERATE SIGNIFICANCE
In **November 2023**, ASIC identified Pepperstone as one of **seven CFD brokers** that breached leverage ratio limits imposed under a 2020 product intervention order. Pepperstone self-reported that a **technical error** allowed some retail clients to trade CFDs with higher leverage than permitted. The company undertook a remediation program to **compensate over 1,500 affected customers** as part of ASIC's broader oversight that resulted in $17.4M+ in total compensation across affected brokers.

#### Insider Trading Scandal (2014) — INDIRECT INVOLVEMENT
In 2014, Pepperstone's **head of sales, Joel Murphy**, was dismissed after discovering a **$7 million insider trading scam** within the company. Two employees (Lukas Kamay and Chris Hill) gained 20,000% in profits using inside information. Murphy reported the scheme to ASIC and his superiors but was **dismissed on the day of the arrests** and denied bonuses exceeding $1 million. This was an internal criminal act by rogue employees, not a company-wide conspiracy. Murphy subsequently founded EightCap (which itself later faced ASIC scrutiny).

#### UK Temporary Suspension (2017)
In **February 2017**, Pepperstone temporarily suspended forex trading for UK clients to comply with FCA obligations. This was a temporary regulatory alignment measure, not a disciplinary action.

### Company Stability Assessment

**Overall Risk Level: LOW**

**Strengths:**
- Operating since 2010 (16+ years track record)
- Privately held with stable management ownership since 2018
- 8 regulatory licenses across multiple jurisdictions
- Consistent profitability in UK subsidiary
- Growing revenue base
- No major enforcement actions from Tier-1 regulators
- Segregated client funds at major banks
- Negative balance protection globally

**Concerns:**
- Japan market exit demonstrates willingness to operate without licenses (2014)
- Brazil CVM action suggests potential for aggressive market entry without full authorization
- Technical error with leverage limits (2023) indicates possible compliance system gaps
- Being private means less financial transparency than publicly listed competitors
- TrustPilot complaints about withdrawal delays and platform stability during volatility

### Financial Stability Indicators

- UK subsidiary profit of £10M+ (FY2023) indicates healthy operations
- Client funds of £25M+ (UK entity alone)
- Significant marketing investment (ATP, F1, UFC sponsorships) suggests strong cash flow
- No reports of capital raising, debt issues, or investor concerns
- Recent launch of Pepperstone Crypto exchange indicates growth capital availability

---

## SUMMARY VERDICT

**Pepperstone is a highly regulated, well-established forex and CFD broker** with an excellent reputation for execution quality and competitive pricing, particularly on the Razor account. The company's 8 regulatory licenses (including 4 Tier-1) place it among the most comprehensively regulated retail brokers globally.

**Key Strengths:**
- Industry-leading regulatory coverage
- Competitive spreads (especially Razor account)
- Zero minimum deposit
- Broad platform selection including TradingView
- Strong brand presence through sports sponsorships
- No inactivity or account maintenance fees

**Key Weaknesses:**
- Historical regulatory issues in Japan and Brazil raise questions about compliance culture
- Platform stability concerns during high volatility (MT4 and mobile app)
- Withdrawal process can be slow with additional verification
- Less transparent than public companies (private ownership)
- No IRESS or direct market access platform

**Best For:** Retail forex and CFD traders seeking a well-regulated broker with tight spreads, multiple platform choices, and global reach. Particularly strong for algorithmic traders and those who use TradingView.

**Caution For:** Traders in jurisdictions served through lower-tier entities (Bahamas, Kenya) have reduced regulatory protection. Traders requiring direct market access or DMA should look elsewhere.
